highlights.item: Payload REST API routes are now mounted from the app root so public forms and auth calls stop failing with 404s.

The homepage instant quote form (with optional scheduling), the custom contact page, and the legacy `/schedule` redirect all align on first-party React Hook Form + Zod routes instead of relying on embedded CRM forms.
The contact path now clearly covers support, billing, refund, privacy, and policy questions, not just pre-quote service inquiries.
The customer portal is now account-first: estimates, invoices, recurring-plan context, service appointments, and account/profile updates are backed by Payload data instead of docs-first placeholders.

Lead submissions still record sync metadata during the create path, but the active product direction is now to keep those records internal while the first-party CRM model replaces third-party providers.

Customer data ownership is shifting toward Payload-native leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, activities, tasks, and service records, with Resend and Stripe reserved for outbound communication and payments.
The ops/dashboard path now uses internal quote, appointment, and service-plan metrics backed by Payload collections instead of provider-specific reads.

Customer activation is now a real phase-05 capability, not just a placeholder workstream: existing CRM customers can request or accept claim links, company accounts can send account-scoped invites from `/account`, and the login surface handles password, magic-link, create-account, and claim/invite entry in smaller modules.

Customer lifecycle delivery now stays first-party too: quote, invoice, and appointment emails can embed the same claim/invite CTA path so activation is part of the normal follow-up flow rather than a separate support step.

next.item: Backfill real customer accounts to invoices, service plans, appointments, and quote `customerUser` links so the portal shows production data instead of only seeded framework records.
Use the now-complete forms, scheduling, and activation foundations as the input layer for phase 07 CRM, billing, and sequence work rather than adding parallel intake systems.
Reseed and verify the expanded contact/support categories plus policy-page links on the public contact route.
Optional next pass: replace remaining layout-builder contact blocks with the same first-party form UI used on `/contact`.
